mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rate levels compress the equity discount rate, raising the present value of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Effective fed funds rate at 0.65% as of March 2020
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with FEDFUNDS below 1% are not significantly positive (mean return <= 0 or t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over a three-month window signals rapid labor market deterioration that crushes consumer demand and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE jumped to 4.4% in March 2020 from 3.5% in February 2020
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales amplify recessionary demand destruction, producing more severe negative S&P 500 returns than either variable alone over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Both INDPRO and RSAFS exhibit pre-recessionary declines in early 2020
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and RSAFS changes has non-negative predictive power (Rank IC >= 0) for 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the immediate aftermath of a demand shock, a rising unemployment rate signals further income loss and demand collapse, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2020 payrolls collapsed and UNRATE rose sharply indicating a demand shock
failure_condition: In periods classified as post-shock recovery, UNRATE increases do not precede negative 5-day returns (mean return >= 0 or hit rate < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production exceeding 2 percent signals a severe supply contraction that compresses corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO index at 103.66 in March 2020 amid factory shutdowns
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered observations during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth below zero percent triggers expectations of an earnings recession, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the economic contraction is priced in.
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2019 GDP at 19221.97 with Q1 2020 expected to print negative
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth does not produce significantly negative 60-day returns (mean return >= 0 or t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales collapse while the federal funds rate is below 1 percent, the inability of monetary policy to offset demand destruction amplifies negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.65% and retail sales falling in March 2020
failure_condition: The joint behavior of RSAFS and FEDFUNDS fails to produce a negative Rank IC (IC >= 0 or not significant at 5% level) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts signals resilient construction activity and consumer confidence, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as cyclical sectors reprice.
evidence_refs: fact:February 2020 housing starts at 1599 thousand units
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST do not precede positive 60-day returns (mean return <= 0 or hit rate < 55%).
